searchDocs.title: isClosed​
searchDocs.content: </> UniPluginConnection.isClosed: boolean Indicates wether or not UniPluginConnection:close() has been called or not.

searchDocs.title: close​
searchDocs.content: </> UniPluginConnection:close() → () Closes this UniPluginConnection. Sets UniPluginConnection.isClosed to true.

searchDocs.title: Automatic Cleanup​
searchDocs.content: You don't need to manually close anything on plugin unload. UniPlugin connects to plugin.Unloading and plugin.Destroying and closes everything — exports, imports, and onClose listeners — automatically.

searchDocs.title: UniPluginConnection​
searchDocs.content: Every connection has two members:  isClosed: boolean — true after close() has been calledclose() — tears down the subscription; safe to call multiple times (subsequent calls are no-ops)  local conn = UniPlugin:exportAs("athar-adv/MyPlugin") print(conn.isClosed) -- false conn:close() print(conn.isClosed) -- true conn:close() -- no-op, no error

searchDocs.title: onClose​
searchDocs.content: Register a callback to fire when the UniPlugin itself is closed (i.e. the plugin unloads or is destroyed):  UniPlugin:onClose(function() toolbar:Destroy() widget:Destroy() end)   You can register as many onClose listeners as you like — all of them fire. The returned connection lets you cancel a listener early if needed:  local listener = UniPlugin:onClose(function() print("closing!") end) -- changed your mind: listener:close()

searchDocs.title: Cleaning Up Imports​
searchDocs.content: Use onImportClosed inside a withImports handler to clean up anything tied to that import group's lifetime:  UniPlugin:withImports({"athar-adv/PluginA"}, function(plugins, onImportClosed) local api = plugins():getApi("main") local rbxConn = api.SomeEvent:Connect(function() ... end) local toolbar = plugin:CreateToolbar("My Toolbar") onImportClosed(function() rbxConn:Disconnect() toolbar:Destroy() end) end)   This fires whenever the import group tears down — whether because a dependency closed or because you closed the returned UniPluginConnection manually.

searchDocs.title: Closing UniPlugin Manually​
searchDocs.content: If you need to shut down your UniPlugin early (e.g. in response to a UI button), call close() directly:  closeButton.Activated:Connect(function() UniPlugin:close() end)   After close(), calling any method on the UniPlugin throws an error. Check UniPlugin.isClosed if you're unsure of its state.

searchDocs.title: Api​
searchDocs.content: </> type Api = {[string]: any} A publicly exported interface for the UniPlugin.

searchDocs.title: plugin​
searchDocs.content: </> UniPlugin.plugin: Plugin  The Plugin this UniPlugin is associated with.

searchDocs.title: apis​
searchDocs.content: </> UniPlugin.apis: {[string]: Api} All registered public apis.

searchDocs.title: isClosed​
searchDocs.content: </> UniPlugin.isClosed: boolean If UniPlugin:close() has been called. If this is true, calling any method will throw an error.

searchDocs.title: exportAs​
searchDocs.content: </> UniPlugin.exportAs( self: UniPlugin, alias: string-- The name to register this plugin under ) → UniPluginConnection-- A connection that can be closed to unregister the export Registers this UniPlugin under alias in the shared exports table, making it discoverable by other plugins calling withImports. Fires the global ExportOpened event so any waiting withImports listeners are unblocked. The returned listener's close removes the export and can be called manually; it is also called automatically when the plugin unloads or is destroyed. Example Usage: local UniPlugin = require(path.to.module)(plugin) UniPlugin:setApi("output", { print = print, warn = warn, error = error }) UniPlugin:setApi("requireInMyContext", { execute = function(module) return require(module) end }) UniPlugin:exportAs("athar-adv/MyPlugin")

searchDocs.title: Errors
searchDocs.content: Type	Description"UniPlugin '{self.plugin.Name}' is closed, cannot call UniPlugin:exportAs(...)"	Occurs if UniPlugin.isClosed is true.

searchDocs.title: withImports​
searchDocs.content: </> UniPlugin.withImports( self: UniPlugin, aliases: {string},-- List of plugin export aliases to wait for handler: ( plugins: () → ...UniPlugin, bindOnImportClosed: (fn: () → ()) → () ) → ()-- Called once all aliases are available; re-runs if the group is torn down and re-established ) → UniPluginConnection-- A connection that can be closed to stop tracking imports Waits for all plugins in aliases to be exported, then calls handler with two arguments: plugins() — returns all resolved UniPlugins in the same order as aliases via multiple returns. bindOnImportClosed(fn) — registers a single callback (overwriting any previous one) that fires whenever this import group is torn down, whether because one of the imported plugins closed naturally or because the returned listener was closed manually. The handler re-runs automatically each time all aliases become available again after a close. Duplicate aliases are silently deduplicated. Passing an empty aliases table is a no-op and returns an inert connection. Close the returned connection to stop listening entirely and tear down the current import group. Example Usage: local UniPlugin = require(path.to.module)(plugin) UniPlugin:withImports({"athar-adv/Jexplorer", "athar-adv/SimpleComplete"}, function(plugins, onImportClosed) local jexplorer, simpleComplete = plugins() local items = {} do local manager = jexplorer:getApi("manager") manager.setSetting("VirtualScrollFrameBuffer", 20) table.insert(items, manager.ExplorerFrame.ClassIconStyleRuleCreated:Connect(function(rule) rule:SetProperty("Image", "rbxassetid://1234") end)) end do local selectionImports = simpleComplete:getApi("selectionImports") selectionImports.triggerAtCurrentPosition() end onImportClosed(function() for _, v in items do v:Disconnect() end end) end)

searchDocs.title: Errors
searchDocs.content: Type	Description"UniPlugin '{self.plugin.Name}' is closed, cannot call UniPlugin:withImports(...)"	Occurs if UniPlugin.isClosed is true.

searchDocs.title: onClose​
searchDocs.content: </> UniPlugin.onClose( self: UniPlugin, fn: () → ()-- Callback to invoke on plugin teardown ) → UniPluginConnection-- A connection that can be closed to cancel the subscription Registers fn to be called when this plugin is torn down (i.e. when the plugin unloads or is destroyed). Multiple listeners can be registered and all will fire on close. The returned listener's close unregisters fn and can be used to cancel the subscription before the plugin closes. Example Usage: local UniPlugin = require(path.to.module)(plugin) UniPlugin:onClose(function() print("Whyd you close my plugin :(") end)

searchDocs.title: Errors
searchDocs.content: Type	Description"UniPlugin '{self.plugin.Name}' is closed, cannot call UniPlugin:onClose(...)"	Occurs if UniPlugin.isClosed is true.

searchDocs.title: setApi​
searchDocs.content: </> UniPlugin.setApi( self: UniPlugin, alias: string?,-- The alias to store the API under. Defaults to "main" api: Api-- The API table to store ) → () Stores api under alias (defaults to "main") on this UniPlugin. Retrieve it from another plugin via getApi. Example Usage: local UniPlugin = require(path.to.module)(plugin) UniPlugin:setApi("output", { print = print, warn = warn, error = error }) UniPlugin:setApi("requireInMyContext", { execute = function(module) return require(module) end }) UniPlugin:exportAs("athar-adv/MyPlugin")

searchDocs.title: Errors
searchDocs.content: Type	Description"UniPlugin '{self.plugin.Name}' is closed, cannot call UniPlugin:setApi(...)"	Occurs if UniPlugin.isClosed is true.

searchDocs.title: getApi​
searchDocs.content: </> UniPlugin.getApi( self: UniPlugin, alias: string?-- The alias to look up. Defaults to "main" ) → Api-- The stored API table Returns the Api stored under alias (defaults to "main"). Errors if no Api has been set for that alias. Example Usage: local UniPlugin = require(path.to.module)(plugin) UniPlugin:withImports({"athar-adv/Jexplorer", "athar-adv/SimpleComplete"}, function(plugins, onImportClosed) local jexplorer, simpleComplete = plugins() local items = {} do local manager = jexplorer:getApi("manager") print(manager.executeJqlQuery("#?player")) local dependencies = jexplorer:getApi("dependencies") print(dependencies.GitApi) end do local editor = simpleComplete:getApi("editor") print(editor.getOpenDocValue().currentImportedModules) end end)

searchDocs.title: Errors
searchDocs.content: Type	Description"UniPlugin '{self.plugin.Name}' is closed, cannot call UniPlugin:getApi(...)"	Occurs if UniPlugin.isClosed is true.

searchDocs.title: close​
searchDocs.content: </> UniPlugin.close(self: UniPlugin) → () Closes this UniPlugin. Cleans up any connections, fires onClose handlers, and makes all methods error on subsequent calls. Example Usage: local UniPlugin = require(path.to.module)(plugin) --[[snip!]] closePluginButton.Activated:Connect(function() UniPlugin:close() end) UniPlugin:onClose(function() for _, v in connections do v:Disconnect() end end)

searchDocs.title: openUniPlugin​
searchDocs.content: </> UniPlugin.openUniPlugin( plugin: Plugin -- The Roblox Plugin instance to wrap ) → UniPlugin Returns the UniPlugin instance for the given plugin, creating it if one doesn't already exist. Only one UniPlugin is ever created per Plugin instance for the lifetime of the studio session. Automatically connects to plugin.Unloading and plugin.Destroying to fire all onClose listeners and close all active withImports and exportAs listeners when the plugin is torn down.

searchDocs.title: Setting an API​
searchDocs.content: setApi stores a table of values on your UniPlugin instance under an alias. Other plugins retrieve it later using getApi with the same alias.  local UniPlugin = require(path.to.UniPlugin)(plugin) UniPlugin:setApi("math", { add = function(a, b) return a + b end, multiply = function(a, b) return a * b end, })   If you only have one API to expose, you can omit the alias entirely — it defaults to "main":  UniPlugin:setApi(nil, { doSomething = function() ... end, }) -- equivalent to: UniPlugin:setApi("main", { doSomething = function() ... end, })

searchDocs.title: Registering the Export​
searchDocs.content: Once your APIs are set, call exportAs with a unique name. Convention is "author/PluginName":  UniPlugin:exportAs("athar-adv/MyPlugin")   This fires a global signal so any plugin already waiting on "athar-adv/MyPlugin" via withImports is immediately unblocked.  Pick a unique alias Because exports live in a shared global table, use a namespaced alias like "your-name/PluginName" to avoid collisions with other plugins.

searchDocs.title: Closing an Export​
searchDocs.content: exportAs returns a UniPluginConnection. You can close it early to unregister the export before the plugin itself unloads:  local exportConn = UniPlugin:exportAs("athar-adv/MyPlugin") -- later... exportConn:close() -- removes the export from the shared table   You don't need to close it manually on plugin unload — UniPlugin handles that automatically.

searchDocs.title: Basic Usage​
searchDocs.content: local UniPlugin = require(path.to.UniPlugin)(plugin) UniPlugin:withImports({"athar-adv/PluginA", "athar-adv/PluginB"}, function(plugins, onImportClosed) local pluginA, pluginB = plugins() local apiA = pluginA:getApi("main") local apiB = pluginB:getApi("utilities") apiA.doSomething() apiB.helperFn() end)   plugins() returns the resolved UniPlugin instances as multiple returns, in the same order as the aliases list.

searchDocs.title: Waiting for Exports​
searchDocs.content: withImports does not require the other plugins to already be loaded. It will wait indefinitely until all listed aliases are exported, then fire the handler. This means plugin load order doesn't matter.

searchDocs.title: Reacting When a Dependency Closes​
searchDocs.content: If one of your imported plugins unloads or is destroyed, the import group tears down and the handler re-runs once all aliases become available again. Use onImportClosed inside the handler to register cleanup:  UniPlugin:withImports({"athar-adv/PluginA"}, function(plugins, onImportClosed) local pluginA = plugins() local api = pluginA:getApi("main") local connection = api.SomeEvent:Connect(function() ... end) onImportClosed(function() connection:Disconnect() end) end)   onImportClosed only accepts one callback at a time — calling it again overwrites the previous one.

searchDocs.title: Getting an API​
searchDocs.content: Use getApi on a resolved UniPlugin to retrieve an API by alias. It errors if no API was set under that alias, so you get a clear message rather than a silent nil.  local api = pluginA:getApi("math") -- explicit alias local main = pluginA:getApi() -- defaults to "main"

searchDocs.title: Stopping an Import​
searchDocs.content: withImports returns a UniPluginConnection. Close it to stop watching entirely:  local importConn = UniPlugin:withImports({"athar-adv/PluginA"}, function(plugins, onImportClosed) ... end) -- later, when you no longer need the import: importConn:close()   Closing it also fires the current onImportClosed callback if one is registered.

searchDocs.title: PluginA — The Provider​
searchDocs.content: PluginA exposes a simple counter API and registers itself under "athar-adv/PluginA".  -- PluginA/init.server.lua local UniPlugin = require(path.to.UniPlugin)(plugin) local count = 0 UniPlugin:setApi("counter", { increment = function() count += 1 end, decrement = function() count -= 1 end, get = function() return count end, }) UniPlugin:setApi("logger", { log = function(msg) print(`[PluginA] {msg}`) end, }) UniPlugin:exportAs("athar-adv/PluginA") UniPlugin:onClose(function() print("PluginA shutting down") end)

searchDocs.title: PluginB — The Consumer​
searchDocs.content: PluginB waits for PluginA, uses its APIs, and cleans up properly when either plugin unloads.  -- PluginB/init.server.lua local UniPlugin = require(path.to.UniPlugin)(plugin) local toolbar = plugin:CreateToolbar("Counter Plugin") local incrementBtn = toolbar:CreateButton("Increment", "Add 1 to counter", "") local decrementBtn = toolbar:CreateButton("Decrement", "Subtract 1 from counter", "") UniPlugin:withImports({"athar-adv/PluginA"}, function(plugins, onImportClosed) local pluginA = plugins() local counter = pluginA:getApi("counter") local logger = pluginA:getApi("logger") local connections = { incrementBtn.Click:Connect(function() counter.increment() logger.log(`Count is now {counter.get()}`) end), decrementBtn.Click:Connect(function() counter.decrement() logger.log(`Count is now {counter.get()}`) end), } onImportClosed(function() for _, c in connections do c:Disconnect() end logger.log("PluginB disconnected from PluginA") end) end) UniPlugin:onClose(function() toolbar:Destroy() end)

searchDocs.title: What Happens at Runtime​
searchDocs.content: Both plugins load. Load order doesn't matter — withImports waits.Once PluginA calls exportAs, PluginB's handler fires immediately.Clicking the buttons calls into PluginA's counter API.If PluginA is reloaded, onImportClosed fires, connections are cleaned up, and withImports starts waiting again automatically.When PluginB unloads, the toolbar is destroyed via onClose.

searchDocs.title: Getting Started
searchDocs.content: Getting Started UniPlugin is a Plugin wrapper that provides simple inter-plugin import/export semantics for public plugin apis. What does this mean? It just means that if 2 plugins, say, PluginA and PluginB, they both use UniPlugin, then they are able to import apis of eachother using UniPlugin:exportAs() and UniPlugin:withImports(), setting their apis using UniPlugin:setApi(). This makes interaction between different Plugins extremely trivial to do, and essentially "unifies" the plugins. Which is where the name "UniPlugin" comes from! "Unified Plugin".
